event_blocks.summary: Aerial drone footage reveals a devastated urban landscape a year after devastating earthquakes in Southeastern Turkey, with collapsed buildings and rubble under somber music, as a narrator describes "ruined cities and disappointed hopes" and hundreds of thousands still residing in temporary shelters. The scene shifts to a large settlement of prefabricated housing units, where a woman hangs laundry, illustrating daily life amidst hardship, while the narrator mentions President Erdogan's unfulfilled promises of rapid reconstruction. A man in a black shirt and gray vest speaks directly to the camera in a desolate area, expressing a mix of hope to "resurrect this place" but also doubt. The narrator highlights that many earthquake survivors, especially children, are haunted by trauma. The video then shifts to a ballet studio where young girls practice under an instructor's guidance, symbolizing resilience, before returning to aerial shots of the ruined city with text overlay 'Disaster of the Century' and 'Turkey One Year After the Earthquake.' The segment concludes with a close-up of a basketball court surrounded by rubble. Later, in prefabricated unit 215, a 'dreary metal box funded by the government,' Seval, in a gray sweater, helps their baby brother Ali brush his teeth while her husband Engin, a teacher, interacts with their daughter Kavin, who is wearing a pink jacket and expresses a desire to "do some ballet." Seval describes the challenges of their living situation, including water outages and the city's broken infrastructure, stating, "You can't wash yourself when you want." Engin and Seval, both teachers, lost their secure life on February 6, 2023. The young girl recounts a terrifying experience during the earthquake: "It was very dark everywhere. I looked around and the wall was cracking. Things were falling in every direction. I thought I was having a nightmare."

Man in black shirt and gray vest.description: A man, identified as Engin, wearing a black t-shirt with a white logo, seen interacting with the young girl and later speaking directly to the camera. His voice briefly adds, 'We didn't hear,' in response to the situation.

Aerial drone footage.description: High-angle shots of ruined buildings, rubble fields, and temporary housing camps, capturing the scale of destruction and recovery efforts, with a female narrator describing the scene as a 'patchwork of ruins and empty lots' a year after 'powerful earthquakes' and 'many neighborhoods are now virtually deserted, uninhabitable'.

Young girl in pink jacket.description: A young girl wearing a red velour tracksuit with 'HEY!' in white sequins, seen writing and later speaking with hands clasped. Her voice recounts a terrifying experience: 'It was very dark everywhere. I looked around and the wall was cracking. Things were falling in every direction. I thought I was having a nightmare.'

Woman in gray sweater.description: A woman, identified as Seval, wearing a gray knitted sweater with 'imperfect' written on it, seen speaking expressively and later holding a toddler. Her voice describes the challenges of their temporary housing, including water outages and broken infrastructure, stating, 'You can't wash yourself when you want... Sometimes I wake my child up at 2 in the morning to wash her because that's when we have water. The city's infrastructure is broken. The authorities can't get things under control. Everything has become so complicated.'

Toddler in white outfit.description: A small child in a white long-sleeved outfit with a textured pattern, being held by the woman and playing with a yellow toy, showing curiosity and engagement in the family setting.

Prefabricated housing unit 215.description: A white modular housing unit, serving as the family’s temporary home, with visible interior features like a mounted TV, washing machine, and kitchenette. Its modest conditions are highlighted by Seval's audio describing water outages and the narrator's explanation of the family's displacement after February 6, 2023.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a sweeping aerial view of a devastated urban landscape, showcasing widespread earthquake destruction with collapsed buildings and rubble, set against a somber, melancholic background music. A female narrator immediately sets the scene, describing "ruined cities and disappointed hopes," explaining that a year after devastating earthquakes in Southeastern Turkey, hundreds of thousands still reside in temporary shelters. The scene transitions to a large temporary settlement of prefabricated housing units, where a woman is seen hanging laundry, illustrating daily life amidst hardship. The narrator mentions President Erdogan's unfulfilled promises of rapid reconstruction as the footage cuts to a crowded scene where a man in a suit, possibly a political figure, is greeted by a crowd. A man in a black shirt and gray vest then speaks directly to the camera in a desolate area, his voice heard in Turkish with an English translation, expressing a mix of hope to "resurrect this place" but also doubt, feeling "we're just fooling ourselves." The narrator continues, highlighting that many earthquake survivors, especially children, are haunted by trauma and long for comfort and normalcy. The video then shifts to a heartwarming scene inside a ballet studio, where young girls in pink tutus practice under an instructor's guidance, symbolizing resilience, as the melancholic music continues. The final frames return to aerial shots of the ruined city, with text overlay reading 'Disaster of the Century' and 'Turkey One Year After the Earthquake,' emphasizing the scale and timing of the tragedy. The video concludes with a close-up of a basketball court surrounded by rubble, as the narrator begins to identify "The Southern Turkish city of Antakya, once home..." before the segment ends.

The video opens with somber, reflective music accompanying aerial drone footage of a vast, devastated urban landscape, which a female narrator describes as a 'patchwork of ruins and empty lots,' a year after being 'battered by powerful earthquakes.' The scene transitions to ground-level shots of damaged structures and a crushed car, with a yellow excavator seen clearing rubble, as the narrator explains that 'excavators are still working to remove the debris' and 'many neighborhoods are now virtually deserted, uninhabitable.' The focus then shifts to a temporary settlement of prefabricated white housing units, where a man, identified by the narrator as Engin Aşkar, walks hand-in-hand with a young girl, his daughter Kavin, in a bright pink jacket. They enter their 'container shelter,' unit 215, which the narrator calls a 'dreary metal box funded by the government.' Inside, the modest living space reveals Kavin's mom, Seval, in a gray sweater, helping their baby brother Ali brush his teeth. Engin and Kavin playfully interact, with Engin's voice heard saying, 'Let's do some ballet, but be careful.' The narrator notes that 'six-year-old Kavin has hardly any room to play' in their 'cramped quarters,' and the family, including Kavin's mom Seval and baby brother Ali, is 'struggling with many other problems,' despite their efforts to establish normalcy, symbolized by a child's drawing labeled 'Küçük Kızım Seval' on the wall.

The video opens with a close-up of a young girl, wearing a red velour tracksuit with 'HEY!' in white sequins, sitting at a wooden table inside a prefabricated housing unit. She writes with a red pencil while a woman, identified as Seval, in a gray sweater with 'imperfect' written on it, speaks to her, gesturing expressively. Seval's voice describes the challenges of their living situation, stating, "You can't wash yourself when you want. There are many water outages. Sometimes I wake my child up at 2 in the morning to wash her because that's when we have water. The city's infrastructure is broken. The authorities can't get things under control. Everything has become so complicated." The modest setting, with a mounted TV, washing machine, and kitchenette, underscores her words. The scene transitions to a wider shot of the family on the floor, where Seval holds a toddler in a white outfit, and a man, identified as Engin, in a black t-shirt, interacts with the young girl. Engin briefly adds, "We didn't hear." A narrator explains that Engin and Seval are both teachers who lost their secure life and plans on February 6, 2023. The camera then focuses on Engin speaking directly to the camera, followed by close-ups of the young girl, her hands clasped, and the toddler chewing on a yellow toy. The young girl's voice recounts a terrifying experience, saying, "It was very dark everywhere. I looked around and the wall was cracking. Things were falling in every direction. I thought I was having a nightmare." This intimate family moment, bathed in natural light within their temporary home, highlights their resilience amidst the aftermath of a traumatic event.

event_blocks.summary: A young girl, initially believing the earthquake was a nightmare, recounts her experience as a 7.8 magnitude quake followed by another massive tremor caused unprecedented devastation in Southeastern Turkey, collapsing hundreds of thousands of buildings due to shoddy construction and lax safety enforcement. The father, Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu, speaking emotionally to the camera, described the terrifying seconds under rubble as feeling like weeks, while his daughter reassured her mother that he would save them. Despite his family's survival, over 53,000 people perished, as evidenced by the vast cemetery in Antakya filled with white grave markers. Abdulrahman, visibly emotional, stood among the graves, holding a photo of his deceased brother and beside the grave of FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU, recounting his 33-hour ordeal trapped under debris and the absence of state aid in the apocalyptic aftermath. The narrative then shifts to the ongoing hardship in a temporary settlement of prefabricated housing units, where residents, including a woman pushing a cart and another carrying laundry, navigate muddy paths, with hundreds of thousands still awaiting their cities' recovery in these shelters, facing harsh winter conditions, as symbolized by a damaged prefabricated unit next to a tent.

Aerial drone footage.description: High-angle shots capture the extensive destruction of urban areas, with collapsed buildings, piles of rubble, and rescue operations involving excavators and emergency vehicles. The footage includes text overlays such as 'File, February 2023' and logos from DW and IHA, contextualizing the scenes as post-earthquake recovery efforts, while a female narrator describes the 7.8 magnitude quake, its devastation, and the blame on shoddy construction.

A man wearing a black t-shirt with a whi.description: The man, now wearing a black puffer jacket over a white t-shirt with 'LUF IAN' printed on it, stands in a cemetery, visibly emotional as he holds a phone showing a photo of a loved one. He speaks solemnly, his expression heavy with grief, and is later seen standing beside a grave marked with the name 'FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU' and the dates 10.08.1972 – 06.02.2023.

Woman carrying laundry.description: A woman in a red skirt and dark top carrying a bundle of white laundry, walking between prefabricated units in the settlement.

Woman in blue scarf.description: A woman with blonde hair wearing a blue scarf and dark jacket, standing beside Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu in the cemetery, listening attentively with a somber expression.

Grave marker for FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU.description: A white marble grave marker inscribed with 'FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU', birth date 10.08.1972, death date 06.02.2023, with a small wooden sign marked '303' placed beside it. This grave is identified as belonging to Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu's deceased brother.

Temporary settlement with prefabricated.description: An expansive area filled with rows of colorful prefabricated housing units, some with blue, red, and white roofs, arranged in grid-like patterns, with vehicles and people moving between them, serving as temporary shelters for hundreds of thousands still holding out a year after the disaster, enduring winter temperatures and rain, as they await their cities' recovery.

Woman pushing cart.description: A woman in a striped top and pink shoes pushing a colorful, striped cart along a wet, muddy path in the temporary settlement.

Damaged prefabricated unit.description: A wooden prefabricated unit with visible damage, surrounded by debris and stacked wood, next to a tent, indicating ongoing hardship and makeshift living conditions.

Man in black puffer jacket.description: Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu, a man with graying hair and a beard, is first seen in a black shirt speaking directly to the camera, then later in a black puffer jacket over a white t-shirt with 'LUF IAN' printed on it. He stands in a cemetery, visibly emotional, holding a phone displaying a photo of his deceased brother, Fatiha Metin Daşçıoğlu. He speaks solemnly in Turkish, expressing grief and recounting his experience of being trapped under rubble for 33 hours, describing the aftermath as an apocalypse where everyone had lost someone and and there was no help from the state. He mourns both his brother and his mother.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a young girl, now in a red velour tracksuit with 'HEY!' in white sequins, sitting calmly and speaking in Turkish, recounting, "I thought I was having a nightmare." The scene abruptly shifts to chaotic, ground-level footage from February 2023, showing people fleeing as a building collapses in a cloud of dust, accompanied by a distant 'whoa' sound. This is followed by frantic rescue efforts amidst rubble, with individuals digging through debris and carrying a body on a stretcher. A female narrator explains that this was not a nightmare, but a 7.8 magnitude quake followed by another massive tremor, causing "unprecedented devastation in Southeastern Turkey." Aerial drone footage then reveals the vast scale of destruction, with entire city blocks reduced to rubble, heavy machinery clearing debris, and temporary housing units scattered among the ruins. The narrator details that "some 300,000 buildings collapsed or were heavily damaged across 11 provinces," blaming "shoddy construction and lax enforcement of safety codes" for the disaster, adding that "within seconds, homes turned into death traps." The video transitions to an indoor setting where a man, identified as the father, in a black t-shirt with a white logo, speaks emotionally to the camera in Turkish, translated by a male narrator who states, "That was the scariest time of my entire life... Those seconds felt like weeks." He is later shown sitting on the floor with the young girl and a woman in a gray sweater with 'imperfect' written on it, who appears to be the mother, as they sit together on cushions in a modest, temporary living space. The father gestures while speaking, recounting how he tried to protect his daughter, and how she kept telling her mum, "It's okay, it's gonna be okay. Daddy will save us."

The segment opens with Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu, a man in a black shirt, speaking directly to the camera in Turkish, expressing a poignant sentiment: "My father will save us. My father is with us. Don't cry, okay?" A female narrator then explains that while his family survived, over 53,000 people did not. The scene transitions to sweeping aerial drone footage of a vast cemetery in Antakya, filled with rows of white grave markers, which the narrator describes as a monument to the immense pain and loss. Abdulrahman, now wearing a black puffer jacket over a 'LUF IAN' t-shirt, is seen standing among these graves, visibly emotional as he holds a phone displaying a photo of a smiling man, identified by the narrator as his deceased brother. He stands beside a grave marked 'FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU' with the dates 10.08.1972 – 06.02.2023, and a small wooden sign '303'. A woman in a blue scarf stands beside him, listening intently as Abdulrahman speaks solemnly in Turkish, recounting his harrowing experience of being trapped under rubble for 33 hours. A male narrator translates his words, describing the aftermath as an apocalypse where "No one knew what to do. Everybody had lost someone, and the state wasn't there for us. There was no help." The scene shifts to an aerial view of a sprawling temporary settlement of prefabricated housing units, followed by ground-level shots of residents navigating wet, muddy paths. A woman in a striped top pushes a colorful cart, and another in a red skirt carries laundry, illustrating daily life in these temporary shelters. The female narrator concludes by stating that a year later, hundreds of thousands in the afflicted region are still holding out in these container blocks or other temporary shelters, defying harsh winter temperatures and rain, as they await their cities' recovery. The final shot shows a damaged wooden prefabricated unit next to a tent, symbolizing the ongoing hardship and displacement.

event_blocks.summary: In a temporary settlement, a woman in a gray corduroy jacket adjusts a blue tarp over her makeshift shelter, expressing despair over lost home and the harsh conditions, while a political figure, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, walks through a crowded tent camp, shaking hands and promising the completion of 319,000 housing units within a year from a podium marked 'AFAD' and 'KIRIKHAN'. Aerial footage reveals a massive construction site with unfinished buildings and cranes, where a construction worker installs a window frame. At another site on the outskirts of Antakya, a woman in a blue scarf and a man, Mustafa Arslan, with a TOKI team, work on thousands of apartments, facing delays attributed to heavy rain, rising costs, and economic uncertainty, with an urban planner estimating a decade or more for rebuilding. A man in a black leather jacket criticizes the sole focus on housing, lamenting the lack of investment in other sectors and the challenge of resettling displaced populations, passing a 'Urban Renewal' billboard. Amidst the ruins and sounds of heavy machinery, a male narrator highlights the 'negligence of the century' and the failure of authorities to address poor building stock, while a green excavator demolishes a building. Engin Aşkar, a man in a black t-shirt and gray vest, points to where his three-story house once stood, expressing the difficulty of envisioning a new beginning and the feeling of self-deception in community efforts to resurrect the area. His young daughter, Kavin, in a pink jacket, walks through the debris, recalling playing in her garden before the earthquakes. The narrator notes Antakya's struggle to recover, with shop owners selling from containers, and concludes with scenes of a phone kiosk and a 'BAKLAVA MARKET' vendor, symbolizing the return of daily life and small businesses amidst ongoing recovery efforts.

Political figure in suit.description: A man in a dark suit, identified as Recep Tayyip Erdogan, shown on a smartphone screen speaking at a podium with microphones, with Turkish text 'SİZLERDEN 1 YIL SÜRE İSTİYORUM' visible, indicating a public address related to recovery efforts.

Podium with AFAD and KIRIKHAN signage.description: A white podium with the AFAD logo and 'KIRIKHAN' label, positioned outdoors with microphones, from which President Erdogan delivers his speech promising new housing units.

Construction worker.description: A man wearing a red vest and gray pants, working on a balcony, installing a window frame with tools, surrounded by construction materials and unfinished walls, symbolizing the ongoing rebuilding efforts.

Aerial drone footage.description: High-angle shots showing a vast construction site with numerous unfinished apartment buildings, cranes, and muddy roads, which a narrator describes as one of many 'construction sites like this one now dotting the earthquake-hit region'.

Woman in gray corduroy jacket.description: Melek Daşkaplan, a woman wearing a gray corduroy jacket over a black top, is seen standing in a makeshift shelter, gesturing while speaking in a distressed Turkish voice. A narrator translates her words, revealing her inability to adapt to her new life, the loss of her home and everything, her despair for her children's future amidst the rain and cold, and her wish that her family had died.

Woman in blue scarf.description: A woman with blonde hair wearing a blue scarf and dark coat, standing beside the man in the gray vest, engaged in conversation, likely a reporter or observer documenting the situation.

Blue redevelopment billboard.description: A large blue billboard with Turkish text 'Yerinde Kentsel Dönüşüm ile EVİN YERİNDE YENİLE' and promotional offers like '750.000 HİBE' and '2 YIL ÖDEMESİZ KREDİ', advertising government-backed urban renewal and home renovation programs.

Man pushing cart with materials.description: A man pushing a two-wheeled cart loaded with construction materials along a paved road, passing the redevelopment billboard, symbolizing the labor and movement within the reconstruction zone.

Man in black leather jacket.description: A man wearing a black leather jacket over a yellow shirt and black scarf, standing in a muddy, debris-filled area with a partially demolished building behind him, speaking emotionally with expressive hand gestures, his scene underscored by narration about the disaster and negligence, likely a local resident affected by the redevelopment.

Construction worker on scaffolding.description: A worker in a blue uniform seen on scaffolding of a concrete building, actively engaged in construction, highlighting the ongoing labor at the site.

Man in black puffer jacket.description: Mustafa Arslan, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a black puffer jacket with a Turkish flag patch and a TOKI logo, identified by the narrator as part of the state housing agency, speaks directly to the camera, stating that rain is the biggest problem but they are working nonstop to move earthquake victims in as soon as possible.

Young girl in pink jacket.description: A young girl wearing a bright pink jacket with a white fur-lined hood and red pants, identified as six-year-old Kavin. Her translated young girl's voice shares a poignant memory of playing in her garden with cousins, now finding 'nothing left' after the earthquakes.

Man in black shirt and gray vest.description: A man wearing a black t-shirt and gray vest, identified as Engin, standing in a rubble-strewn area. His translated male voice expresses his biggest dream for a solid roof and his skepticism about the community's ability to resurrect the place, feeling they are 'just fooling ourselves'.

Woman in blue headscarf.description: A woman wearing a blue headscarf and long dark dress, walking through the muddy construction site, carrying a white bag, possibly a local resident navigating the area.

Phone kiosk vendor.description: A man inside a small kiosk with orange signage reading 'EFSAN UMUT İLETİŞİM HER ÇEŞİT TELEFON KILFLARI', selling phone accessories, indicating commercial activity resuming in the area.

Baklava vendor.description: A man at a green market stall labeled 'BAKLAVA MARKET', cutting and serving baklava, with a close-up of his hands using a metal scraper, symbolizing the return of daily life and small businesses.

Yellow Komatsu excavator.description: A large yellow Komatsu excavator model PC 380 LC actively demolishing a collapsed building, surrounded by rubble and debris, indicating ongoing demolition and reconstruction efforts.

raw_segments: The segment begins with Melek Daşkaplan, a woman in a gray corduroy jacket and black top, adjusting a blue tarp over her makeshift shelter in a temporary settlement. Her distressed Turkish words, translated by a narrator, convey her inability to adapt to her new life, having lost her home and everything, expressing despair for her children amidst the rain and cold, and even wishing her family had died, as the camera pans to her cluttered outdoor kitchen. The scene then shifts to a crowded tent camp where President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, a political figure in a dark suit, walks through, shaking hands and engaging with survivors. A narrator explains that shortly after the quakes and facing a national election, Erdogan made bold promises, which he reiterates from a podium marked 'AFAD' and 'KIRIKHAN' to a large crowd. His Turkish speech, translated, promises the completion of 319,000 housing units within a year. The video transitions to aerial footage of a massive construction site with numerous unfinished apartment buildings, cranes, and muddy roads, identified by the narrator as one of many new sites in the earthquake-hit region, followed by a close-up of a construction worker installing a window frame on a balcony, symbolizing the ongoing rebuilding efforts.

The video opens with a woman in a blue scarf and dark coat, standing beside Mustafa Arslan, a man in a black jacket with a Turkish flag patch and TOKI logo, at a large construction site. As the woman points toward unfinished buildings, a female narrator explains that the project, on the outskirts of Antakya, is going much slower than promised, with Mustafa Arslan and his TOKI team racing to complete thousands of apartments. The camera pans across the site, revealing multiple apartment buildings in various stages of construction with visible workers. Mustafa Arslan then speaks directly to the camera, identifying the biggest problem as the heavy rain in the region, but assures that they are working nonstop to ensure earthquake victims can move in quickly. The narrator continues, noting that experts attribute delays not just to weather, but also rising construction costs and chronic economic uncertainty in Turkey, with urban planner Serkan Koç estimating a decade or more to rebuild cities like Antakya. The scene shifts to a man in a black leather jacket and yellow shirt, standing in a muddy, debris-filled area with a partially demolished building behind him. Speaking expressively with hand gestures, he criticizes the current focus solely on housing construction, lamenting the lack of serious investment in tourism, the service sector, or agriculture, and the challenge of bringing back the displaced 300-400,000 people. A man is briefly seen pushing a cart past a large blue billboard advertising 'Urban Renewal in Place, Renew Your Home in Place,' before the video returns to the man in the leather jacket, continuing his emotional testimony against the backdrop of destruction and rebuilding.

The segment opens with a wide shot of a partially demolished multi-story building in a muddy, debris-strewn post-disaster reconstruction zone, as a male narrator states that people 'were driven out, we have to rebuild this city economically as well.' A man in a black leather jacket and a woman in a blue scarf walk, then stop to examine a smartphone displaying a video of political figure Recep Tayyip Erdogan speaking at a podium, with Turkish text 'SİZLERDEN 1 YIL SÜRE İSTİYORUM' (I am asking for one year from you). A female narrator warns that 'the urban planner warns against repeating past mistakes. Many homes collapsed because safety codes had been ignored,' by 'builders and public officials responsible for greenlighting building projects,' while the sound of heavy machinery is heard. The scene cuts to a broader view of the devastated landscape under an overcast sky, with heavy machinery and damaged buildings. The man in the black leather jacket then speaks directly to the camera, gesturing expressively in front of the ruins, as a male narrator adds, 'We've not only experienced the disaster of the century here, but also the negligence of the century.' He continues, 'If the central and local authorities had done something sooner about the poor stock of buildings, and if there had been more serious inspections, we would not have lost so many lives in this city.' The camera pans to show a green excavator actively demolishing a building, its mechanical sounds prominent. The scene shifts to a different location within the same bleak, muddy area, where Engin Aşkar, a man in a black t-shirt and gray vest, walks with his young daughter Kavin, in a bright pink jacket. Engin points toward the horizon, stating in Turkish, 'Ev şuradaydı bak,' translated as 'Look, this used to be our house, three floors,' and later, 'Oraya park ederdik,' translated as 'We always parked the car over there,' as Kavin looks around curiously at the scattered rubble, temporary structures, and distant residential buildings under a gray sky, conveying a sense of displacement and recovery efforts.

The video opens with a close-up of Engin, a man wearing a black t-shirt and gray vest, standing in a desolate, rubble-strewn area. A female narrator introduces him, noting that despite government promises, it's difficult for him to envision a new beginning. Engin, speaking in a translated male voice, shares his deepest desire: 'My biggest dream right now is to have a solid roof over our heads again, but it looks like it will take a long time.' He further reflects on the community's efforts, stating, 'We often tell each other that we will resurrect this place. But every time we say that, I feel like we're just fooling ourselves.' The scene then shifts to Kavin, a young girl in a bright pink jacket with a white fur-lined hood and red pants, walking through the debris, looking around curiously. The narrator identifies her as six-year-old Kavin, for whom the situation 'still feels very much unreal.' Kavin, speaking in a translated young girl's voice, poignantly recalls, 'I used to play in our garden a lot with my cousins. But after the earthquakes, there's nothing left.' The man and a woman with blonde hair wearing a blue scarf and dark coat are shown conversing in the same devastated landscape. The setting is bleak, with scattered debris and makeshift structures under an overcast sky. The scene transitions to a construction site where a yellow Komatsu excavator is actively demolishing a collapsed building, with a woman in a blue headscarf walking nearby. The narrator explains that 'Antakya is a city struggling to get back on its feet,' and 'many shop owners are now selling from containers.' The final scenes shift to a more urban environment, showcasing a small kiosk selling phone accessories with Turkish text 'EFSAN UMUT İLETİŞİM HER ÇEŞİT TELEFON KILFLARI' and a green market stall labeled 'BAKLAVA MARKET', where a vendor is cutting baklava, symbolizing the return of daily life and small businesses amidst recovery efforts, as the narrator concludes that 'the economy here is on its...'

event_blocks.summary: Amidst the aftermath of disaster, vendors in an outdoor market express profound struggles with rising costs and lack of work, yet maintain hope for their city's recovery, with one stating rebuilding could take "10 or 15 years." A mother of four, a beverage stand vendor, fights for her children, finding solace in the fact that they are "still alive." Meanwhile, a man identified as Engin drives with his young daughter, anticipating a "fun" day, symbolizing a return to normalcy. In a ballet studio, a man ties his daughter's ballet slippers, helping her overcome post-quake fears. The studio, which previously served as an emergency shelter, now hosts young ballet students, some orphaned by the disaster, led by 'Teacher Fatma,' who uses art and love to help them heal. The instructor notes the children's initial fear and distraction, contrasting with their current focused practice. A man in a black shirt and gray vest emphasizes the need for community and government support for the city's revival, stating, "for there to be life in a city, there must first be people." He and others find the dance lessons a vital "break" from their grief, allowing them a moment to "take a deep breath and forget all our troubles."

Young girl in pink jacket.description: A young girl wearing a bright pink jacket with a white fur-lined hood and red pants, seated in the front passenger seat of a car driven by Engin, then walking hand-in-hand with him, suggesting a close bond and shared journey towards normalcy.

Woman in gray hooded coat.description: A woman wearing a gray hooded coat with a black scarf, speaking into a DW microphone at an outdoor beverage stand with kettles and string lights in a wet environment. She identifies herself as a mother of four, fighting for her children, and emphasizes the importance of holding on and finding hope, stating in Turkish, translated, 'At least we are still alive. That means there is hope.'

Man in black shirt and gray vest.description: A man wearing a black t-shirt and gray vest, standing in a hallway and speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. He provides commentary in Turkish, with an English voice-over, discussing the importance of community, resilience, and government support for a city's recovery, stating that the dance classes offer a much-needed 'break' to 'take a deep breath and forget all our troubles for a moment' after a year of grieving.

Man in patterned sweater and beanie.description: A man wearing a gray beanie and a black and gray patterned sweater, seen arranging produce at a market stall and being interviewed by a DW reporter, with crates of tomatoes and bananas visible behind him. He expresses the difficulties faced by vendors, stating in Turkish, translated, 'We're facing many difficulties. Everything is expensive. There's no work. That's why we're struggling.'

Beverage stand vendor.description: A woman in a gray hooded coat and black scarf, standing at an outdoor beverage stand with kettles and string lights, speaking into a DW microphone, indicating she is a vendor or local resident sharing her story.

Phone kiosk vendor.description: A bald man with a beard, wearing a red shirt and blue jacket, being interviewed outside a small kiosk with an orange sign reading 'iPhone', with construction debris and a white car in the background. He, or another vendor, speaks about the need to make ends meet and the long rebuilding process, estimating it will take '10 or 15 years' for the city to recover.

Man in black jacket with green stripes.description: A man in a black jacket with a green-lined hood, smiling while being interviewed in front of a green and yellow striped wall, possibly a market stall, suggesting he is a local resident or vendor.

Ballet studio environment.description: A brightly lit dance studio with wooden floors, mirrored walls, purple curtains, a visible clock, and shelves with shoes, creating a structured and colorful space for children’s ballet training.

Ballet instructor.description: A woman in an orange tank top and patterned leggings, leading a ballet class with expressive gestures and clear instruction, heard speaking in Turkish to the children, saying 'Bak şimdi bir şey söyleyeceğim size gel' (Look, I'm going to tell you something now, come).

Young ballet students.description: A group of young girls in pink leotards and tutus, practicing ballet movements with focused expressions, their arms raised and feet in ballet slippers, mimicking the instructor’s guidance, and heard playfully speaking in Turkish, 'Gülümse, nerede?' (Smile, where?).

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of fresh produce at an outdoor market, including leafy greens and cabbages, as a man in a beanie and patterned sweater arranges the goods. A female narrator explains that most of their former customers are either "dead or displaced." The man, likely a vendor, is then interviewed by a DW reporter, stating in Turkish, translated, "We're facing many difficulties. Everything is expensive. There's no work. That's why we're struggling." The scene shifts to a bald man with a beard, the phone kiosk vendor, being interviewed outside his kiosk with an 'iPhone' sign, amidst construction debris. He, or another vendor, continues in Turkish, translated, "We decided to do something on our own to make ends meet. Life has to go on. We hope that our city will soon be back on its feet," adding that rebuilding could take "10 or 15 years." A smiling man in a black jacket is also interviewed in front of a green and yellow striped wall, reflecting the shared sentiment of struggle and hope. Next, a woman in a gray hooded coat and black scarf, a beverage stand vendor, speaks into the DW microphone at her stand with kettles and string lights in a wet, outdoor setting, declaring in Turkish, translated, "I am a mother of four children. I'm fighting for them here. We have to hold on somehow. At least we are still alive. That means there is hope." The final scenes show a man, identified by the narrator as Engin, in a black t-shirt and gray vest, driving a car with a young girl in a bright pink jacket beside him, accompanied by the sound of the car engine. Engin expresses optimism, saying in Turkish, translated, "Today will be so much fun," as they later exit the car and walk hand-in-hand down a tree-lined street past a music and dance academy, symbolizing a return to daily life and enduring hope in the recovering community.

The segment opens with a close-up of a young girl's feet as a man in a black t-shirt and gray vest carefully ties her ballet slippers, an action contextualized by a male narrator explaining he's helping his 6-year-old daughter, Kavin, overcome fears after quakes by taking her to her favorite place. Upbeat, orchestral music with a choir-like sound then swells. The scene transitions to a brightly lit dance studio with mirrors and purple curtains, where a woman in an orange tank top and patterned leggings, identified by the narrator as 'Teacher Fatma' from 'Fatma Satan's Ballet School,' leads a group of young girls in pink leotards and tutus. Her clear, instructional voice is heard giving commands like 'Follow me' and counting in Turkish, which is translated to English as 'Three,' 'Four,' and 'Five,' with faint children's voices echoing. The narrator reveals that some of these young ballet students were orphaned by a disaster, and Teacher Fatma, who herself lost relatives and friends, understands their trauma. The instructor demonstrates precise arm movements, which the children mimic with focused expressions and delicate footwork. Later, Teacher Fatma speaks directly to the camera in Turkish, translated to English, explaining that initially, most of the children didn't smile, were constantly distracted, and were too scared to leave their families. She shares how they slowly helped them overcome their fears through art, love, and working together, as the children continue practicing in the background.

The video opens in a vibrant ballet studio with pink and purple walls, mirrored surfaces, and wooden floors, where a Ballet instructor, dressed in an orange tank top and patterned leggings, actively engages with a group of Young ballet students. A female voice is heard expressing the desire for the children to "have a bit of their old lives back," followed by the instructor's clear Turkish instructions to the children, "Bak şimdi bir şey söyleyeceğim size gel" (Look, I'm going to tell you something now, come). As the children in pink leotards and tutus practice ballet movements, raising their arms and following her lead, a narrator explains that the school building withstood recent quakes and was first used as an emergency shelter before resuming classes a few months ago. The lively studio also features the playful voices of children. The scene transitions to a hallway where a group of adults observe. Here, a Man in a black shirt and gray vest steps forward, speaking directly to the camera with a serious yet composed demeanor. He articulates in Turkish, with an English voice-over, that "for there to be life in a city, there must first be people," emphasizing the need for people to come together and for government support. A narrator adds that "we've all been grieving for a year now," a sentiment echoed by the man who states they are "trying to get back up, trying to stay strong." The narrator then highlights that "the dance lessons are helping the parents too," a point reinforced by the man who describes the classes as "a break for us," allowing them to "finally take a deep breath and forget all our troubles for a moment." The setting remains indoors, with framed artwork, indicating a structured and supportive environment for dance education.

event_blocks.summary: A man in a black t-shirt and gray vest speaks in Turkish, uttering "ediyoruz," before a female narrator begins describing a ballet class in a brightly lit studio. Young girls in pink leotards and tutus, guided by a woman in an orange tank top and patterned leggings, practice ballet, mimicking her movements with focused expressions and raising their arms in unison, with close-ups revealing costume details like purple flower embellishments. The narrator states that for these children, these are the happiest moments, a break from the nightmare of the quakes. The studio environment includes mirrored walls, purple curtains, and a visible clock. The scene then shifts to an aerial drone view of a vast construction site with unfinished apartment buildings, cranes, and construction vehicles on muddy terrain, accompanied by the drone's whirring sound. Rows of prefabricated housing units and a blue redevelopment billboard with Turkish text are visible in the background. The narrator describes this as a small break from the strange new life in their ruined city. On-screen text identifies the producers and DW News branding, highlighting the contrast between the ballet class and the devastated urban landscape.

Ballet studio environment.description: A brightly lit dance studio with wooden floors, mirrored walls, purple curtains, a visible clock, and shelves with shoes, creating a structured and colorful space for children’s ballet training.

Blue redevelopment billboard.description: A large blue billboard with Turkish text 'Yerinde Kentsel Dönüşüm ile EVİN YERİNDE YENİLE' and promotional offers like '750.000 HİBE' and '2 YIL ÖDEMESİZ KREDİ', advertising government-backed urban renewal and home renovation programs, seen within the drone footage of the "ruined city."

Young ballet students.description: A group of young girls in pink leotards and tutus, practicing ballet movements with focused expressions, their arms raised and feet in ballet slippers, mimicking the instructor’s guidance, identified by the narrator as "Kavina and the other children" experiencing "the happiest moments" as they dance.

Ballet instructor.description: A woman in an orange tank top and patterned leggings, leading a ballet class with expressive gestures and clear instruction, while a narrator describes the children's activity as "dancing, having fun, and forgetting the nightmare of the quakes."

On-screen text overlay.description: A blue banner at the bottom of the screen displaying 'Producer: Marc Erath', 'Executive Producer: Katharina Kroll', 'DW NEWS', 'dw.com | @dwnews', providing production credits and media branding, appearing silently at the end of the segment.

Man in black shirt and gray vest.description: A man wearing a black t-shirt and gray vest, standing in a hallway and speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, providing commentary in Turkish, specifically saying "ediyoruz," before an English voiceover takes over.

Aerial drone footage.description: High-angle shots showing a vast construction site with numerous unfinished apartment buildings, cranes, and muddy roads, indicating large-scale rebuilding efforts in a post-disaster area, accompanied by the distinct whirring sound of a drone, as a narrator describes the scene as a "strange new life in their ruined city."

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a black t-shirt and gray vest, standing in a hallway and speaking directly to the camera in Turkish, uttering "ediyoruz," before a female narrator's English voiceover begins. The scene then transitions to a brightly lit ballet studio where a group of young girls in pink leotards and tutus practice ballet under the guidance of a woman in an orange tank top and patterned leggings. The narrator describes this activity as "Dancing, having fun, and forgetting the nightmare of the quakes," highlighting that "For Kavina and the other children, these are the happiest moments." The children mimic their instructor's expressive movements, raising their arms and stepping in unison with focused expressions, with close-ups revealing delicate costume details like purple flower embellishments. The studio features mirrored walls, purple curtains, and a visible clock. The scene abruptly shifts to an aerial drone view of a vast construction site, revealing several unfinished apartment buildings amidst muddy terrain, with cranes and construction vehicles visible, accompanied by the distinct whirring sound of the drone. The narrator continues, calling this "A small break from the strange new life in their ruined city." Rows of prefabricated housing units are seen in the background, alongside a blue redevelopment billboard displaying Turkish text promoting urban renewal. On-screen text identifies the producer as Marc Erath and the executive producer as Katharina Kroll, along with DW News branding and social media handles, underscoring a narrative of resilience and recovery through the stark contrast between the innocence of the ballet class and the devastation of the construction site.

global_entity_library.Young girl in pink jacket: A young girl wearing a bright pink jacket with a white fur-lined hood and red pants, seated in the front passenger seat of a car driven by Engin, then walking hand-in-hand with him, suggesting a close bond and shared journey towards normalcy.
global_entity_library.A man wearing a black t-shirt with a whi: The man, now wearing a black puffer jacket over a white t-shirt with 'LUF IAN' printed on it, stands in a cemetery, visibly emotional as he holds a phone showing a photo of a loved one. He speaks solemnly, his expression heavy with grief, and is later seen standing beside a grave marked with the name 'FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU' and the dates 10.08.1972 – 06.02.2023.
global_entity_library.Woman carrying laundry: A woman in a red skirt and dark top carrying a bundle of white laundry, walking between prefabricated units in the settlement.
global_entity_library.Woman in blue scarf: A woman with blonde hair wearing a blue scarf and dark coat, standing beside the man in the gray vest, engaged in conversation, likely a reporter or observer documenting the situation.
global_entity_library.Grave marker for FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU: A white marble grave marker inscribed with 'FATİHA METİN DAŞÇIOĞLU', birth date 10.08.1972, death date 06.02.2023, with a small wooden sign marked '303' placed beside it. This grave is identified as belonging to Abdulrahman Daşçıoğlu's deceased brother.
global_entity_library.Temporary settlement with prefabricated: An expansive area filled with rows of colorful prefabricated housing units, some with blue, red, and white roofs, arranged in grid-like patterns, with vehicles and people moving between them, serving as temporary shelters for hundreds of thousands still holding out a year after the disaster, enduring winter temperatures and rain, as they await their cities' recovery.
global_entity_library.Woman pushing cart: A woman in a striped top and pink shoes pushing a colorful, striped cart along a wet, muddy path in the temporary settlement.
global_entity_library.Damaged prefabricated unit: A wooden prefabricated unit with visible damage, surrounded by debris and stacked wood, next to a tent, indicating ongoing hardship and makeshift living conditions.
global_entity_library.Man in black puffer jacket: Mustafa Arslan, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a black puffer jacket with a Turkish flag patch and a TOKI logo, identified by the narrator as part of the state housing agency, speaks directly to the camera, stating that rain is the biggest problem but they are working nonstop to move earthquake victims in as soon as possible.
global_entity_library.Podium with AFAD and KIRIKHAN signage: A white podium with the AFAD logo and 'KIRIKHAN' label, positioned outdoors with microphones, from which President Erdogan delivers his speech promising new housing units.
global_entity_library.Construction worker: A man wearing a red vest and gray pants, working on a balcony, installing a window frame with tools, surrounded by construction materials and unfinished walls, symbolizing the ongoing rebuilding efforts.
global_entity_library.Woman in gray corduroy jacket: Melek Daşkaplan, a woman wearing a gray corduroy jacket over a black top, is seen standing in a makeshift shelter, gesturing while speaking in a distressed Turkish voice. A narrator translates her words, revealing her inability to adapt to her new life, the loss of her home and everything, her despair for her children's future amidst the rain and cold, and her wish that her family had died.
